summary: Gruber announces the prizes for Daring Fireball's 2005 membership drive, listing software licenses and goods donated by prominent Mac indie developers and companies. The prizes total over $3,000 in value and include offerings from Panic, Bare Bones, 37signals, Rogue Amoeba, and many others. He outlines the rules: members who joined or renewed after June 2005 are eligible, winners are selected randomly via Perl script, and members can optionally rank their prize preferences by email. The post is essentially a promotional announcement to encourage last-week signups before the October 31 deadline.
key_points: Over $3,000 in prizes donated by prominent Mac software companies and indie developers
The prize list reads as a who's-who of mid-2000s Mac indie software: Panic, Bare Bones, Rogue Amoeba, 37signals, Flying Meat, Shirt Pocket, and others
Winners are selected randomly using a simple Perl script, with each member winning only once
Members can optionally email a ranked preference list to increase their chances of getting a prize they want
money_quotes: This is somewhere north of US$3,000 in prizes; my sincere thanks to all of the above companies for their support of Daring Fireball.
Winners will be selected randomly, using a simple Perl script.
key_insight: The breadth and generosity of prizes from Mac indie developers illustrates the tight-knit ecosystem around Daring Fireball and the mid-2000s Mac software community.
